author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Wired GPU kernels into backward passes — 2 changes in 2 files:

**1. reduce_grad_to_shape GPU fast path (arithmetic.rs)**
Added f32 CUDA fast path using sum_axis_f32 to reduce broadcast axes entirely on GPU. Previously downloaded entire gradient to CPU, iterated to sum along broadcast axes, and re-uploaded. Now: clone_buffer + iterative sum_axis_f32 per axis, zero CPU roundtrips. This is the highest-impact fix — called by every AddBackward, SubBackward, MulBackward, DivBackward with broadcast shapes.

**2. LinearFusedBackward bias gradient (linalg.rs line 838)**
Replaced CPU roundtrip (grad_output.cpu() + manual loop + to(device)) with single sum_axis_f32(go_h, &[m,n], 0) call. Bias gradient now stays entirely on GPU.

**Verified no change needed:**
- DivBackward: already uses op-level div/neg/mul in no_grad which dispatch to GPU kernels. Only CPU roundtrip was in reduce_grad_to_shape, now fixed.
- MmBackward: already has mm_backward_gpu, confirmed working.
- MvBackward/DotBackward: skipped per instructions.

All 26 unit tests + 3 doc-tests pass.
